scenarios.invariant: A 128-request idempotent burst creates one case and one execution while every read returns the same durable identity.
Every duplicate or out-of-order delivery schedule produces one legal terminal attempt and cannot duplicate proof.
Adapter reconstruction preserves one case, job, outbox intent, private artifact, and bundle hash across retries.
An unavailable dependency or capability remains fail closed and never falls back to memory, anonymous access, or host execution.
One expired lease is recovered once, publishes one recovery intent, and exhausts only at the bounded attempt limit.
Queued work and pending outbox age cross their warning thresholds deterministically without exposing scoped identifiers.
A private artifact write or verification failure prevents terminal success and cannot manufacture a verified bundle.
Sandbox create, command, snapshot, cancellation, or cleanup failure remains bounded, fail closed, and cleaned or quarantined.
